Article.ArticleTitle: Combined endurance and resistance training in participants with muscle dystrophies: a randomized controlled trial.

Abstract.AbstractText: Muscular Dystrophies (MD) are genetic neuromuscular disorders characterized by a progressive loss of muscle mass and impaired muscle function. This study aimed to determine whether a group-based supervised exercise training program could improve functional capacity and other secondary physical variables in participants with MDs.

patients were randomly allocated to one of the study groups: intervention (IG) or control (CG). Intervention consisted of endurance and resistance training at low-to-moderate intensity twice weekly during 12 weeks as well as a home-based respiratory muscle training program. Main outcome was change in physical functioning based on the Time Up and Go Test (TUG) after 12-week.

45 participants were screened and 38 were finally randomized (19 per arm). Participants in the IG showed a significant but not clinically relevant improvement in the TUG (Z score -2.173, p = 0.030). Both groups improved peripheral muscle strength, being improvements larger in IG compared to CG for quadriceps strength (p = 0.009). CG participants showed a worsening of current fatigue at 12 weeks compared to no change in IG (p = 0.003).
combined endurance and strength supervised exercise training is feasible and safe in participants with MD but limited effectiveness is observed to improve functional and quality of life outcomes.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Department, Hospital Clinic de Barcelona, Barcelona, Spain.

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: A 12-week supervised training (aerobic, strength and endurance) program is safe, feasible, and well-accepted (with high adherence).The intervention helps to stabilize fatigue levels, as fatigue worsened in controls but remained unchanged in the intervention group.Combined exercise has a potential role in mitigating fatigue in muscular dystrophy patients.The training can improve the TUG and quadriceps extensors force, so it eventually should be applied in clinical practice.
